message:
fix(normalize): suppress clippy too_many_lines lint on strip_sudo

Add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] attribute to the strip_sudo() function
in src/normalize.rs. This function handles comprehensive parsing of sudo
command-line options including:

- Simple boolean flags: -E, -H, -n, -k, -K, -S, -b, -i, -P, -A
- Flags with required arguments: -u <user>, -g <group>, -h <host>, -p <prompt>
- The -- terminator handling for positional arguments

The function's length is justified by the complexity of sudo's option parsing,
where splitting into smaller functions would reduce readability without
providing meaningful abstraction. The current monolithic implementation
provides clear, linear control flow through the option parsing logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
